rulings.Case Title: BRENDA BESDANSKY VS. STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT STEVE & IRMA ASTI, IT., LLC, RAYMOND SCARABOSIO, WILLIAM SCARABOSIO, NICHOLAS SCARABOSIO, JACKSON GROUP PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. Notice Of Motion Of Defendants Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425 16 Anti Slapp Motion To Strike The Complaint Of Brenda Besdansky Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Ani-SLAPP Motion is GRANTED as to the portions of the Complaint subject to this motion (Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Motion 3:2-4:4). The claims arising out of service of an "eviction notice" (e.g. Complaint, paragraph 33) incorporated into every cause of action and expressly additionally stated as the basis for relief in causes of action for retaliation, violation of SFRO 37.9, violation of civil code 1940.2, fraud, and unfair business practices fall within the first prong of the ANTI-SLAPP. Plaintiff was provided an opportunity to submit a supplemental brief to show probability of prevailing on the merits by identifying evidence that supports each element of the claim arising out of the service of eviction notice alone (as opposed to other wrongful conduct alleged in the Complaint). Plaintiff's supplemental brief, after listing the required elements for each cause of action, references evidence to only some, but not all, of the elements. Therefore, Plaintiff failed to carry her burden to show probability of prevailing on the merits on the claims arising solely out of the service of the "eviction notice." This ruling has no impact on other claims within the same causes of action. =(501/CFH)

By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Federal Cares Act does not prohibit service of notices of the types involved in this action. =(501/CFH)
